## Structure

UDP S→C 0x03/0x30 — `ShortPlayerInfo`. Compact player-info
broadcast — used when the client has already received the
detailed `LongPlayerInfo` (0x03/0x25) and just needs to update
the visible name + ID. Verified 2026-05-10 against
`ShortPlayerInfo.java` source matching all 18 retail samples.

```
[0..1]    map_id LE16              zone/map ID
[2..3]    00 00                    CONSTANT padding
[4..7]    player_id LE32           target player's character UID
[8..N]    name CString             ASCII null-terminated
```

Sample retail bytes (catalog inner_data):
```
02 00 00 00  6b cb 01 00  4b 72 61 73 6e 69 79 20 53 6f 6b 6f 6c 00
^map=0x0002  ^player_id=0x0001cb6b  ^"Krasniy Sokol\0"

03 00 00 00  00 cc 01 00  53 6f 6b 6f 6c 69 74 6f 00
^map=0x0003  ^player_id=0x0001cc00  ^"Sokolito\0"

01 00 00 00  09 c5 01 00  41 64 72 69 61 6e 6f 20 52 61 6f 6e 00
^map=0x0001  ^player_id=0x0001c509  ^"Adriano Raon\0"
```

## Variants

Single packet form. Body length varies (13-22B in retail) based
on character name length. The structure is otherwise rigid.

## Observed contexts

Emitted when a player becomes visible / spawns in (ShortPlayer
form) — typically follow-up to `LongPlayerInfo` for nearby
players. Concentrated in PvP-heavy captures
(`VEHICLE_DRONE` × 6, LONG_PARTY × 3 each).

The 6 captures that emit this packet all involve multiple
players in proximity. The 11 captures that don't emit it are
solo-play sessions.

## Open questions

- Why does the server emit BOTH `LongPlayerInfo` (0x03/0x25,
  61-86B with full appearance) AND `ShortPlayerInfo`
  (0x03/0x30, 13-22B with name + ID only)? Possibly:
  - LongPlayer for first-time visibility (full state).
  - ShortPlayer for re-acquaintance (already-seen player
    re-entering visibility).

## Server-side handler

`server.gameserver.packets.server_udp.ShortPlayerInfo` — emits
the verified body. Constructed with `(Player pl, PlayerCharacter
pc, int mapId)` triplet.

Per `lstplayer_error_misattribution.md` memory: the
"LSTPLAYER : Update Message corrupted Size:12 32" client error
is NOT from this packet (it's byte-correct). The real culprit
is `0x03/0x2d` sub-action `0x11`.

Tested via existing parity audit. Wired from player-spawn
broadcast paths as the lighter-weight alternative to
`LongPlayerInfo`.
